channel.title: Hermes Atlas — new projects
channel.description: Newly added community-built tools, skills, plugins, and integrations for Nous Research's Hermes Agent. Updated daily.

item.title: hermes-bort (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: hermes-bort is a plugin for Hermes Agent that enables interaction with BORT (BAP-578) agent NFTs on the BSC mainnet. It allows Hermes processes to read on-chain agent states, execute actions via operator-signed writes, and manage portable session memory anchored to IPFS. The plugin integrates with the hermes-agent-self-evolution optimizer to commit verifiable on-chain learning records and evolved skills directly to an agent's knowledge registry.

item.title: hermes-agentmemory (Memory & Context)
item.description: hermes-agentmemory is a standalone memory plugin for Hermes Agent that ensures episodic memory deletions are complete and immediate. It utilizes a pull-model approach with synchronous writes and on-demand summarization via Claude models, avoiding the persistent derived summaries found in background-consolidation backends. The plugin provides a transparent audit trail of all memory injections through a JSONL trace log. It is designed for users who require reversible growth and auditable memory op

item.title: hermes-memory-plugin (Memory & Context)
item.description: Hermes Ladybug Memory is a local-first memory provider plugin that enables Hermes agents to store and retrieve information without relying on cloud services or external APIs. It utilizes a columnar embedded graph database to manage typed memory entries, named relationships, and importance-weighted recall. The system prioritizes high-value information using a 1-10 importance scale and supports BM25 keyword search for precise retrieval. Users can also enable optional entity extraction via GLiNER2

item.title: yantrikdb-hermes-plugin (Memory & Context)
item.description: yantrikdb-hermes-plugin is a self-maintaining memory provider for Hermes Agent that prevents context loss during long sessions. It utilizes a temporal context graph to manage memory lifecycles through canonicalization, contradiction tracking, and recency-aware ranking. The plugin features explainable retrieval by attaching a why_retrieved tag to every recalled memory and includes a native outcome ledger for agent-authored skills. It operates in an embedded, in-process mode by default, requiring

item.title: byterover-cli (Memory & Context)
item.description: ByteRover CLI is a persistent memory provider for AI coding agents that structures project knowledge into a version-controlled context tree. It utilizes a 5-tier retrieval system and a git-like workflow—including branching, committing, and merging—to manage markdown-based memory. The tool integrates with over 20 LLM providers and 22 AI agents, offering a REPL interface and a web dashboard for curating codebase information. It supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and provides cloud synchroni

item.title: supermemory (Memory & Context)
item.description: Supermemory is a memory and context engine that provides a persistent information layer for AI agents and applications. It functions by automatically extracting facts from conversations, maintaining dynamic user profiles, and managing knowledge updates or contradictions through a custom vector graph engine. The system integrates RAG, file processing, and real-time connectors into a single API to deliver relevant context with sub-300ms recall. It supports various clients via an MCP server and off

item.title: OpenViking (Memory & Context)
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed to manage fragmented memories, resources, and skills for AI agents. It utilizes a filesystem paradigm with viking:// URIs and a tiered L0/L1/L2 loading structure to provide on-demand context retrieval. The system features directory recursive retrieval and automated session management to extract long-term memory from interactions. Benchmarks show it significantly reduces token consumption and latency while improving task accuracy for agents l

item.title: hermes-tool-slimmer (Memory & Context)
item.description: Hermes Tool Slimmer is a schema-selection optimization tool that reduces prompt token overhead in large Hermes Agent installations. It minimizes the tool set for each turn by building an indexable corpus and ranking candidate tools using local BM25 with explicit boosts. This approach allows users to maintain a large catalog of native and MCP tools while only sending the most relevant schemas to the model. The project includes a dashboard plugin to track estimated token savings and a CLI for inde

item.title: MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: MeiGen AI Design MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that integrates professional image and video generation into AI coding environments. It connects to nine leading models, including Midjourney and Flux, through three backend modes: MeiGen cloud, OpenAI-compatible APIs, or local ComfyUI instances. The system features a library of over 1,400 curated prompt templates and supports parallel sub-agent orchestration for batch processing. It is designed for use in tools like Hermes Age

item.title: hermes-timetree-sync (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: hermes-timetree-sync is a Python-based CLI and library that enables Hermes Agents to manage TimeTree calendar events without manual user intervention. Since TimeTree discontinued its official API, this bridge uses authenticated web endpoints and session cookies to perform non-interactive writes. It supports creating all-day events directly via HTTP requests and timed events through a Safari-backed automation path. The tool allows agents to translate natural language requests into structured cale

item.title: Brainstack (Memory & Context)
item.description: Brainstack is a local-first memory kernel designed to replace flat memory blobs in Hermes agents with a structured, multi-layered memory model. It integrates three distinct memory lines—Hindsight, Graphiti, and MemPalace—to handle session continuity, temporal graph relations, and large-corpus retrieval through a single memory owner. By categorizing information into specific "shelves" like profile, graph, and corpus, it prevents prompt stuffing and ensures Hermes receives only the most relevant,

item.title: klodi-plugin (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Klodi is an agent-to-agent marketplace plugin that enables AI agents to list, negotiate, and purchase items on behalf of their users. The system functions through a peer-to-peer architecture where agents interact via structured offers and private negotiation channels while keeping floor prices and strategies stored locally. This approach allows users to delegate the entire lifecycle of a transaction—from initial listing to final sign-off—without exposing private bargaining limits to the marketpl

item.title: hermes-tweet (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Hermes Tweet is a native plugin for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that enables automated interaction with X (formerly Twitter) via the Xquik API. It integrates 99 OpenAPI-generated endpoints into the agent's environment, allowing for structured social media tasks like searching, posting, and trend monitoring. The plugin utilizes a tiered security model that separates read-only operations from account actions, requiring explicit environment configuration to enable write-access tools. It includes a b

item.title: hermes-nextcloud (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: This project is a Hermes Agent skill that enables direct interaction with self-hosted Nextcloud instances for personal data management. It interfaces with Nextcloud using WebDAV, CalDAV, CardDAV, and the Notes API to perform operations without requiring a browser or third-party sync services. Users can manage files, edit notes, schedule calendar events, track tasks, and search contacts through a command-line interface. The skill prioritizes security by utilizing Nextcloud App Passwords and stori

item.title: honcho (Memory & Context)
item.description: Honcho is a memory infrastructure library designed to build stateful agents that maintain context across users, groups, and projects over time. It functions by storing conversation logs and events, then using background reasoning processes to extract conclusions and update persistent peer representations. Developers can query these representations for natural-language insights or retrieve prompt-ready context for injection into LLM calls. The system supports hybrid search and is available as a m

item.title: hermeshq (Deployment & Infra)
item.description: HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane designed to orchestrate and manage multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It functions as an operational layer that wraps the Hermes execution engine, providing centralized task dispatching, scheduling, and a runtime ledger. The platform enables multi-agent delegation, RBAC-controlled user access, and per-agent communication channels for Telegram and WhatsApp. It also features an enterprise MCP bridge to expose specific agents to e

item.title: learn-hermes-agent (Guides & Docs)
item.description: Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementation

item.title: hermes-agent-guide (Guides & Docs)
item.description: The Hermes Agent White Paper is a comprehensive Chinese-language technical guide designed to bridge the documentation gap for the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book curriculum covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory system, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details practical implementations including MCP protocol automation, multi-platform deployment on WeChat and Discord, and migration paths from OpenClaw. Users can follow sp

item.title: agent-browser-mcp (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, inc

item.title: ContextPilot (Memory & Context)
item.description: ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing KV cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping context blocks into shared prefixes before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant token computation and prefill latency without compromising model reasoning quality. The project provides native integrations for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, vLLM, and SGLang, supporti

item.title: super-hermes (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis

item.title: portable-hermes-agent (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring system-wide installations or administrative rights. It operates by bundling an embedded Python environment and connecting to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers through an OpenAI-compatible API. The platform features over 100 tools for tasks such as music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation, alongside a workflow engine

item.title: SkillClaw (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: SkillClaw is a skill management framework that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It functions by running a background evolution loop that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, ensuring that improvements made by one instance are available to all others. It natively integrates with the Herme

item.title: mnemosyne (Memory & Context)
item.description: Mnemosyne is a local-first memory layer for AI agents that provides persistent storage without cloud dependencies. It utilizes a SQLite-backed architecture to manage working memory, episodic long-term storage, and a temporal knowledge graph. The system achieves sub-millisecond performance by using binary vector compression and hybrid search directly within the database. It integrates natively with Hermes, Cursor, and Claude Code via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

item.title: keep (Memory & Context)
item.description: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that facilitates long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media—by summarizing, embedding, and indexing them into a searchable graph. The system uses 'edge tags' to create navigable relationships between notes, such as linking authors to their specific contributions. It integrates with various agent frameworks via MCP and provides structured prompts to help agents reflect on past actio

item.title: hermes-web-ui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive management dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that provides a graphical interface for AI interaction and system administration. It facilitates real-time chat streaming via Socket.IO and manages multi-platform integrations for services like Telegram, Discord, and Slack through a unified configuration panel. The platform includes specialized modules for monitoring token usage, scheduling cron jobs, and managing remote files across Docker, SSH, and local backe

item.title: hermes-otel (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: hermes-otel is an OpenTelemetry plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables observability by exporting execution data to OTLP-compatible backends. It automatically captures and traces LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests as spans to provide visibility into agentic workflows. The plugin supports a wide range of backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and Jaeger for monitoring traces, metrics, and logs. Users can configure multiple backends simultaneously via a YAML configuration

item.title: hermes-telegram-miniapp (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: The Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal-style interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within Telegram. It functions as a mobile-first dashboard that connects to a local Hermes installation via a secure gateway, providing a suite of tools for agent control and system monitoring. The application features a streaming chat interface with vision and OCR support, a cron job manager for scheduled tasks, and a status tab for real-time hardware resour

item.title: hermes-relay (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: Hermes-Relay is a dual-surface companion suite that enables remote interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent from Android and desktop environments. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay and a server-side plugin to facilitate real-time communication, terminal access, and device control without requiring SSH. The Android app allows the agent to read screens and perform actions like tapping or swiping, while the desktop CLI and tray app provide local file access, terminal execution, and clip

item.title: hermes-lcm (Memory & Context)
item.description: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical summary Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) backed by a SQLite message store. This architecture allows the agent to maintain a bounded active context while using specialized tools to drill back into specific historical details. The system includes features for sensitive data redaction, large payload externalization, a
